title: Reverse Linked List
statement: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list and return the new head. Solve it in-place by rewiring next pointers.

steps.description: State the goal in terms of pointers: each node's next should point to its previous node, so the original head becomes the tail.
Identify the three pointers needed during iteration: prev, curr, and a saved next.
Walk through the rewire sequence per node: save curr.next, point curr.next to prev, advance prev=curr and curr=saved.
Handle the empty-list and single-node edge cases (return head unchanged in both).
State complexity: O(n) time, O(1) space.
